notes: Codex ends its turn with a single clarifying question (literal `?`). The daemon classifies the live rollout session via the trailing-? text path (session.IsWaitingForUserInput â†’ state_classifier.go decision 2a): at the turn's task_complete (turn_done), IsAgentDone() is true AND the last assistant message ends in `?`, so the session transitions workingâ†’waiting instead of workingâ†’ready. Codex has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool (matrix prior); this is the adapter-agnostic text classifier. Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI: headless `codex exec` is single-shot and exits at turn end (process_exited race, see basic-turn spec), so the waiting episode would be lost. The interactive driver (drive-codex-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout session alive after the turn â€” the REPL is torn down only at script end â€” and a trailing sleep lets the waiting episode settle before teardown. No user reply is sent, so the arc is readyâ†’workingâ†’waiting and the session stays waiting until process_exited; there is no waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready recovery to assert. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then working, then waiting.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt â†’ working on the rollout session
Agent's reply ends in a question â€” turn ends in waiting, not ready
The waiting episode is visible and persists â€” no skip-over to ready while the agent is gated on the user
Driver tears down the REPL while the session is still waiting â€” process_exited on the rollout session
